top_signals.title: UAE's exit from OPEC+ reduced the group's share of crude oil production and capacity
top_signals.summary: On April 28, 2026, the United Arab Emirates (UAE) announced that it was leaving OPEC, effective on May 1. OPEC was formed in 1960 by Iraq, Iran, Kuwait, Saudi Arabia, and Venezuela, with the stated objective to "coordinate and unify petroleum policies among Member Countries." OPEC is best known for its effect on global crude oil prices.

top_signals.title: The Importance of the Battle of the Little Bighorn
top_signals.summary:
On June 25, 1876, Lt. Col. George Armstrong Custer and an entire battalion of the 7th Cavalry Regiment rode to their deaths and into American legend on the ridges north of Montana’s Little Bighorn River. The battle was decided in a few hours. Its meaning remains contested 150 years later.Little Bighorn stands alongside Yorktown, Gettysburg, and D-Day in the pantheon of iconic American battles. But it is a curious addition there, in that it was a small engagement, fought by just a few hundred men on a compact piece of terrain. More curiously still, it was a loss. Not just

The post The Importance of the Battle of the Little Bighorn appeared first on War on the Rocks.

top_signals.title: Apple Prices Climb and the AI Trade Roars Back | Open Interest 6/25/2026
top_signals.summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest."The AI trade is roaring back after Micron’s blowout forecast. JPMorgan shakes up succession plans for Jamie Dimon. Ford CEO Jim Farley weighs in on AI versus experience, and Apple raises prices as the chip shortage hits. Plus, Bob Sloan on market positioning risks and Adam Hetts on why earnings still matter. (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

alerts.title: Luxury spending now driven by experiences and 'inheritourism'
alerts.summary: While luxury goods sales are expected to grow between 1% and 4%, experiences are on track for growth of between 3% and 7% this year, according to a new report.

alerts.title: Burnham camp goes quiet on hospitality VAT cut
alerts.summary: Allies of Prime Minister-to-be Andy Burnham have gone quiet on whether he would slash VAT for hospitality despite previously backing calls to do so and committing to a raft of other measures to support pubs, restaurants and bars. The outgoing Mayor of Greater Manchester had previously said he would back calls to cut value-added tax [...]
